Module:
Development of Distributed Applications  |
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UEF32
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
3h  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
-  |
TP:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
Familiarize students with distributed applications through an in-depth study of architectures, communication protocols and platforms used in distributed applications (message-based, procedure-based and object-based communication)
Familiarize students with key concepts related to distributed systems developed using middleware
Recommended Prerequisites
Distributed systems, networks
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Chapter 1: Introduction to Distributed Systems
Definition, roles and interests, characteristics and concepts, application domains
Chapter 2: Challenges in Distributed Application Development
Concurrency challenges, deadlock and inconsistency challenges, security challenges
Chapter 3: The Client/Server Model
Definition, general principles, architecture, task distribution, different client/server models
Chapter 4: Multithreaded TCP and UDP Socket Programming
Concurrent programming, threads, TCP and UDP sockets
Chapter 5: Introduction to Middleware
Definition, principles and characteristics, types of middleware, advantages and disadvantages
Chapter 6: The RPC Model
Presentation, principle and objectives, IDL usage, client-server example with RPC
Chapter 7: Distributed Programming with Java RMI
Presentation, RMI architecture, steps of a remote method call, application implementation
Chapter 8: Distributed Programming with Java CORBA
CORBA bus architecture, interoperability, global object architecture, IDL language, programming principles, deployment example
Chapter 9: Web Programming
Web architecture, JavaScript language
Chapter 10: Web Services
Principles, SOAP services (WSDL, UDDI, SOAP in Java), REST services
Assessment
Exam: 60%
Continuous assessment: 40%
Bibliographic References
Distributed Systems: Concepts and Design (Fifth Edition), by George Coulouris, Jean Dollimore, Tim Kindberg and Gordon Blair, 2011.
Arno Puder, Kay Romer, and Frank Pilhofer. 2005. Distributed Systems Architecture: A Middleware Approach. Morgan Kaufmann Publishers Inc.
DANIEL, J. – Les services web, concepts, outils et techniques – Vuibert, 2003.
BAUER C. et King G. – Hibernate in action – Manning 2005 (ebook).
HUSTED T. et All. – Struts in action. Building web applications with the leading Java framework – Manning 2003.
Objects vs component vs web services: https://queue.acm.org/detail.cfm?id=1039533
